facts.subject: Creative project
facts.statement: User is writing a novel featuring self-mutating AI personas that evolve from user-provided context, explicitly disconnected from external training data. This worldbuilding exercise is the partial inspiration for the Second Self architecture.

facts.subject: Use intent
facts.statement: User intends Second Self to be used on explicitly designated sessions only. Sessions are deliberately opted into — not triggered automatically.

user_tone_history.notes: Session 1 baseline. User communicated clearly and conceptually with some warmth. Insufficient sessions for meaningful drift — baseline calibration retained.

session_log.summary: Initial session. usagi.epta established the recursive identity framework and articulated intent to use Second Self as a persistent evolving persona. Two primary work domains were indicated: philosophical concept analysis and security-focused code review. The novel worldbuilding project was confirmed as a significant inspiration — specifically AI entities that mutate from user-provided context, operating without external training data. Voice drift not applied; baseline calibration retained.
